note_data.title: Trigonometry
keyPoints: Trigonometrical functions include sine, cosine, and tangent
Trigonometrical functions can be defined for angles of any size
The sine and cosine graphs have specific characteristics
The tangent graph has distinct properties
Equations can be solved using graphs of trigonometrical functions
Circular measure is used for angle measurement
Arc length of a circle can be calculated using trigonometry
Area of a sector of a circle involves trigonometrical calculations
Other trigonometrical functions exist beyond sine, cosine, and tangent

howToAnswer.instruction: Show each step algebraically using known identities. Start with one side and manipulate to reach the other side.
Find principal solution first, then use periodicity to find all solutions in the given range.
Give the exact numerical answer. Show working for partial marks.
Rewrite in the required form showing all steps clearly.

modelAnswer.question: Solve the equation 2sin²x - 3cosx + 3 = 0 for 0° ≤ x ≤ 360°.
modelAnswer.answer:
Using sin²x = 1 - cos²x:
2(1 - cos²x) - 3cosx + 3 = 0
2 - 2cos²x - 3cosx + 3 = 0
2cos²x + 3cosx - 5 = 0
Let y = cosx: 2y² + 3y - 5 = 0
(2y + 5)(y - 1) = 0
y = -5/2 or y = 1
cosx = -5/2 (impossible as -1 ≤ cosx ≤ 1)
cosx = 1, therefore x = 0°, 360°
markBreakdown: Mark 4: Reject impossible solution and give final answers

formulas: sin(A ± B) = sinA cosB ± cosA sinB
cos(A ± B) = cosA cosB ∓ sinA sinB
a sinx + b cosx = R sin(x + α) where R = √(a² + b²)
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: CAST diagram showing signs of sin, cos, tan in four quadrants: All positive in first quadrant, Sin positive in second, Tan positive in third, Cos positive in fourth
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q8 (6 marks) — they asked students to find the angle of elevation from a point 50m from the base of a tower to the top, given the tower height as 30m
This topic appeared in O/N 2022 P1 Q12 (3 marks) — students had to calculate the third side of a triangle using the cosine rule given two sides and the included angle
This topic was examined in M/J 2022 P2 Q6 (5 marks) — candidates were required to find the area of a triangle using the sine rule formula given two sides and the included angle
This topic featured in O/N 2023 P1 Q15 (4 marks) — students needed to solve a bearing problem involving finding distance using sine and cosine ratios

workedExample.question: A ladder of length 5m leans against a vertical wall. The foot of the ladder is 3m from the base of the wall. Calculate the angle the ladder makes with the ground. (4 marks)
steps: Step 1: Draw a right-angled triangle with hypotenuse = 5m (ladder), adjacent side = 3m (ground distance)
Step 2: Identify which trigonometric ratio to use. We have adjacent and hypotenuse, so use cosine
Step 3: Write the equation: cos θ = adjacent/hypotenuse = 3/5 = 0.6
Step 4: Find the angle: θ = cos⁻¹(0.6) = 53.1° (to 1 decimal place)
workedExample.answer: The angle the ladder makes with the ground is 53.1°
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct diagram/identification of triangle, 1 mark for choosing cosine ratio, 1 mark for correct substitution, 1 mark for final answer to appropriate accuracy

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused sine and cosine ratios when the triangle was oriented differently' — always identify which side is opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse relative to the angle you're finding
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to check if their calculator was in degree mode' — always verify calculator settings and check if your angle answer is reasonable for the context

Examiner Report 2023: 'Candidates often rounded too early in multi-step problems' — keep full calculator values until the final step, then round to the specified number of decimal places or significant figures
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must master drawing and labeling right-angled triangles with the angle clearly marked, and all three sides labeled as hypotenuse (longest side, opposite the right angle), opposite (side facing the angle in question), and adjacent (side next to the angle in question, but not the hypotenuse). Include the angle symbol and ensure measurements/variables are clearly marked on appropriate sides.
note_data.quickRecap: The trigonometry chapter in Cambridge A Level Mathematics covers trigonometrical functions for all angle sizes, their graphs, and applications to solving equations. It includes circular measure, calculations for arc length and sector area, and extends to other trigonometrical functions beyond the basic sine, cosine, and tangent.
